info.name: Fyredocs API - Complete Collection
info.description:
Complete API testing collection for Fyredocs backend services. Includes authentication, file upload, PDF conversion, organization, and optimization endpoints.

All endpoints return a standardized response format:
{
  "success": true/false,
  "message": "...",
  "data": { ... } or null,
  "error": { "code": "...", "details": "..." } or null,
  "meta": { "requestId": "...", "page": N, "limit": N } or null
}

For detailed API documentation, visit /docs on the running server.

exec: // Add X-Request-ID header to all requests

request.description: Check if the API Gateway is running
Create a new user account. Sets HTTP-only access_token cookie (8-hour lifetime).

response.name: 201 - User Created

request.description: Login with existing credentials. Returns user info and sets HTTP-only access_token cookie.
Get currently logged-in user information. Requires valid access_token cookie.

response.name: 200 - Current User

request.description: Get detailed user profile including phone and image.

response.name: 200 - User Profile

request.description: Refresh the access token. Requires a valid existing access_token cookie.

Request a password reset link. Always returns 200 — the response is identical whether or not the email is registered. Rate-limited 3/min per IP. When RESEND_API_KEY is unset on the server, the link is logged via slog instead of emailed.
Consume a single-use reset token and set a new password. Revokes every active session for the user and denylists outstanding access tokens. Rate-limited 5/min per IP.
Logout and clear authentication cookie. Adds token to denylist for immediate revocation.

item.name: 2. File Upload
item.description: Presigned S3 multipart upload protocol. File bytes go browser → object storage directly via presigned URLs; the API only brokers the session. Flow: init → PUT each part to its presigned URL (collect ETags) → complete → use uploadId in a job-creation request.

request.description: Create an S3 multipart upload and receive presigned PUT URLs for every part. The declared fileSize is checked against the plan limit (413 FILE_TOO_LARGE) before any URL is issued.

PUT the part bytes DIRECTLY to the presigned URL from init (saved as {{part_url}}). The bytes go to object storage, not the API. Save the ETag response header for the complete request. Re-presign expired URLs via 'Get Part URLs'.

query.description: Comma-separated part numbers; omit for all parts

request.description: Re-presign part upload URLs to resume an interrupted upload or refresh expired URLs.

Finalize the multipart upload from the collected part ETags. The part count must equal totalParts (400 UPLOAD_INCOMPLETE otherwise). The true object size is re-verified against the plan limit (413 FILE_TOO_LARGE; the object is deleted).
Session metadata for an in-progress upload.
Cancel an in-progress upload: aborts the S3 multipart upload (freeing stored parts) and clears the session. Idempotent — returns 204 even for unknown/expired sessions.
One-release migration stub for the retired chunk-streaming protocol. Always returns 410 Gone with code UPLOAD_PROTOCOL_CHANGED. Will be removed next release.

response.name: 410 - Protocol Changed

request.description: Convert Word document (.doc, .docx) to PDF using LibreOffice
Convert Excel spreadsheet (.xls, .xlsx) to PDF using LibreOffice
Convert PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx) to PDF using LibreOffice
Convert HTML document (.html, .htm) to PDF using LibreOffice
Convert multiple images to single PDF (one image per page). Supports: .jpg, .png, .gif, .webp, .bmp
Check the status of a conversion job. Status: pending, processing, completed, failed
302-redirects to a 5-minute presigned object-storage URL that serves the converted PDF with attachment disposition. Follow the redirect to download the bytes.
Delete a conversion job and its files
Get all jobs for word-to-pdf tool
Convert PDF to Word (.docx) using LibreOffice
Convert PDF to Excel (.xlsx) using LibreOffice
Convert PDF to PowerPoint (.pptx) using LibreOffice
Convert PDF pages to PNG images (returns ZIP file) using pdftoppm
Convert PDF to HTML with images (returns ZIP file) using pdftohtml
Extract plain text from PDF (.txt) using pdftotext
Convert PDF to PDF/A-2b archival format using Ghostscript
Check the status of a PDF conversion job
302-redirects to a 5-minute presigned object-storage URL that serves the converted file with attachment disposition. Follow the redirect to download the bytes.
Delete a conversion job and its files
Get all jobs for pdf-to-word tool
Merge multiple PDFs into a single document using pdfcpu
Split PDF into individual pages or ranges (returns ZIP file). Range format: "1-3,5,7-9" or "all"
Remove specified pages from PDF. Format: "2,4,6" or "2-5"
Extract specified pages into a new PDF. Format: "1,3,5-7"
Reorder pages in PDF according to specified order. Format: "3,1,2,4"

Convert images to PDF with the full scanner options: OCR (Tesseract, languages eng/deu/fra/spa/hin), pageSize (auto/a4/letter/id — ignored when ocr=true), enhance (none/grayscale/bw/color-boost), and per-page corners (normalized 0..1 perspective-warp quad, e.g. from Detect Edges) + rotation (0/90/180/270, clockwise, applied after the warp). pages[] is index-aligned with the input files. All options are optional and validated at job creation (400 INVALID_OPTIONS).

Detect the document quad in a completed image upload (mobile scanner assist). The upload is peeked at, not consumed — the same uploadId stays valid for the subsequent Scan to PDF job. Corners are normalized 0..1 against the decoded pixel grid and can be passed into scan-to-pdf options.pages[i].corners. When no document is found, full-image corners with confidence 0 are returned (still 200). Rate-limited per IP (RATE_LIMIT_DETECT, default 60/window). Errors: 400 INVALID_INPUT (unknown uploadId or PDF upload), 422 relayed upstream code (e.g. UNDECODABLE_IMAGE, IMAGE_TOO_LARGE), 502 DETECTION_UNAVAILABLE.
Check the status of an organization job
302-redirects to a 5-minute presigned object-storage URL that serves the organized PDF with attachment disposition. Follow the redirect to download the bytes.
Delete an organization job and its files
Get all jobs for merge-pdf tool
Compress PDF to reduce file size. Quality options: screen (72dpi), ebook (150dpi), printer (300dpi), prepress (high quality)
Fix corrupted or damaged PDFs using Ghostscript to rebuild the PDF structure
Add searchable text layer to scanned PDFs using Tesseract OCR. Options: language (default: eng), dpi (default: 300)
Check the status of an optimization job
302-redirects to a 5-minute presigned object-storage URL that serves the optimized PDF with attachment disposition. Follow the redirect to download the bytes.
Delete an optimization job and its files
Get all jobs for compress-pdf tool
Get the authenticated user's job history across all tools and services. Requires valid authentication.
List the caller's documents. Optional filters: status, folderId, tagId, q (full-text), trashed, orgId; paging page/limit (max 100). Auth required.
Create a document (metadata only; bytes live in object storage). organizationId is optional (editor+).
Get one owned document with its tags.
Update name/folderId/status/metadata. Empty folderId moves to root.
Soft-delete (move to Trash).
Restore a soft-deleted document from Trash.
Hard-delete a document and its tag associations.
Attach a tag to a document.
Detach a tag from a document.
File a future job's output into an org workspace (editor+). Empty organizationId clears the hint.
List folders. Optional parentId filter.
Create a folder.
Rename or move a folder.
Soft-delete a folder; contained documents move to root.
List the caller's tags.
Create a tag (idempotent on user+name).
Delete a tag and its associations.
List the caller's exports (status/metadata, no bytes).
Queue an async CSV/JSON export of documents in scope.
Poll export status (queued|processing|ready|failed).
Download a ready export artifact.
List orgs the caller belongs to, each with the caller's role. Paged: page (default 1), limit (default 25, max 100); returns meta{page,limit,total}.
Create an org; caller becomes owner.
Get an org the caller belongs to (with the caller's role).
List members of the org. Paged: page (default 1), limit (default 25, max 100); returns meta{page,limit,total}.
Add or update a member (admin+). Role: admin|editor|viewer.
Change a member's role (admin+, not the owner).
Remove a member (admin+, never the owner).
Recent notifications (max 50) + unreadCount.
SSE live bell. Subscribes to core-NATS notify.<userId>. Use a client that keeps the connection open.
Mark all of the caller's notifications read.
Mark one notification read.

item.name: 11. Admin Analytics

request.description: Lightweight authorization probe. Returns 200 for super-admins, 401/403 otherwise. Does no work; used by the Caddy edge forward_auth to gate the embedded Grafana observability dashboards (/grafana/*).

NATS/JetStream health for the admin dashboard. Scrapes the internal NATS monitoring endpoint (/varz + /jsz). Requires a super-admin session. Returns server info, streams, consumers, and a summary (including DLQ depth).

item.description: Super-admin-only analytics/observability endpoints (routed to analytics-service via /admin).
